skill.name: prompt-optimization
skill.description: Helps design, review, and improve prompts for LLM features, AI agents, system prompts, few-shot examples, and structured outputs.
skill.summary: Optimize prompts for LLM applications, agents, and AI workflows.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown fences and prompt-writing prose. No evidence found of executable commands, cryptographic code, network access, file access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Detected As Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at these lines. The locations are Markdown code fences and prompt examples, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences or prompt text in SKILL.md. No command interpreter, script file, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Detected As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm usage at these lines. The locations contain metadata or prompt optimization prose, not crypto code or APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language about prompts and AI workflows. No hashing, cipher, certificate, or cryptographic library usage appears in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time when prompts produce vague, inconsistent, or hard-to-test results. This skill gives structured techniques for clearer prompts, examples, constraints, and output formats.
actual_capabilities: Designs prompts with clear roles, tasks, constraints, examples, and output formats.
Improves prompts for LLM features, agents, system prompts, and AI workflows.
Adds few-shot examples to demonstrate desired style and behavior.
Breaks complex tasks into reasoning steps and verification points.
Provides review criteria for security, performance, maintainability, and best practices.
limitations: Does not run prompt evaluations or measure model performance directly.
Does not include scripts, test harnesses, datasets, or automation files.
Requires the user to provide task context and target model behavior.
Cannot guarantee identical results across different LLM providers or model versions.

use_cases.title: Improve Product AI Features
use_cases.description: Turn rough feature prompts into structured instructions with roles, constraints, examples, and predictable response formats.

use_cases.title: Create Agent System Prompts
use_cases.description: Define agent responsibilities, boundaries, workflow steps, and output expectations for more reliable behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Prompt Patterns
use_cases.description: Document reusable prompt patterns for code review, analysis, planning, and structured reporting tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Basic Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this prompt for clearer results: [paste prompt]. Keep the original goal, add missing context, and suggest a better output format.

prompt_templates.title: Create a System Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a system prompt for [agent or assistant type]. Include role, scope, constraints, refusal boundaries, and response format.

prompt_templates.title: Add Few-Shot Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this task with two or three few-shot examples: [describe task]. Show input, expected output, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an AI Workflow Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for ambiguity, missing constraints, unsafe behavior, and evaluation gaps: [paste prompt]. Provide a revised version and rationale.

output_examples.input: A vague request to make an assistant review code.
output: A clearer reviewer role with defined scope and priorities.
Specific review criteria for security, performance, maintainability, and best practices.
A structured report format with severity, location, explanation, and suggested fix.

output_examples.input: A prompt that gives inconsistent answers for a classification task.
output: A rewritten task statement with explicit category definitions.
Few-shot examples that show desired decisions and edge cases.
A concise output format that reduces ambiguity.

output_examples.input: A system prompt for an agent that lacks boundaries.
output: A defined role and operating scope.
Clear constraints for unsupported requests and uncertain inputs.
Verification steps before the agent produces final results.

best_practices: Provide the original prompt, target user, expected output, and known failure cases.
Ask for examples and edge cases when the task depends on style or judgment.
Test revised prompts against real inputs before adopting them in production.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic improvement without explaining the desired behavior.
Do not use hidden constraints that are missing from the prompt text.
Do not rely on chain-of-thought wording when a concise rationale is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It improves prompts for LLM applications, AI agents, system prompts, few-shot learning, and structured outputs.

faq.question: Does it execute code or run evaluations?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and provides prompt design guidance, examples, and review patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt patterns are general and can be adapted for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and similar LLM tools.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the current prompt, target task, target users, output requirements, and examples of poor results.

faq.question: Does it include prompt injection defenses?
faq.answer: It can help define boundaries and review unsafe behavior, but it is not a complete security testing framework.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only Markdown and prose content, with no executable scripts, network calls, or secret access.
